channel.title: Super Micro Computer, Inc. — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Super Micro Computer, Inc. (supermicro).

item.title: CVE-2025-12007 · 8.4 HIGH — Supermicro BMC firmware update validation bypass
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the Supermicro BMC firmware validation logic at Supermicro MBD-X13SEM-F . An attacker can update the system firmware with a specially crafted image.

item.title: CVE-2025-12006 · 7.2 HIGH — Supermicro BMC firmware update validation bypass
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the Supermicro BMC firmware validation logic at Supermicro MBD-X12STW-F . An attacker can update the system firmware with a specially crafted image.

item.title: CVE-2025-8727 · 7.2 HIGH — A stack buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the Supermicro BMC Web function(SSL).
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the Supermicro BMC web function at Supermicro MBD-X13SEDW-F. After logging into the BMC Web server, an attacker can use a specially crafted payload to trigger the Stack buffer overflow vulnerability.

item.title: CVE-2025-8404 · 5.5 MEDIUM — Stack buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the Supermicro BMC Shared library
item.description: Stack buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the Supermicro BMC Shared library. An authenticated attacker with access to the BMC exploit stack buffer via a crafted header and achieve arbitrary code execution of the BMC’s firmware operating system.

item.title: CVE-2025-8076 · 7.2 HIGH — A stack buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the Supermicro BMC Web function
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the Supermicro BMC web function at Supermicro MBD-X13SEDW-F. After logging into the BMC Web server, an attacker can use a specially crafted payload to trigger the Stack buffer overflow vulnerability.

item.title: CVE-2025-7623 · 5.4 MEDIUM — Supermicro BMC SMASH services has a Stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability
item.description: Stack-based buffer overflow in the SMASH-CLP shell. An authenticated attacker with SSH access to the BMC can exploit a stack buffer overflow via a crafted SMASH command, overwrite the return address and registers, and achieve arbitrary code execution…

item.title: CVE-2025-7704 · 5.4 MEDIUM — Supermicro BMC SMASH services has a Stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability
item.description: Supermicro BMC Insyde SMASH shell program has a stacked-based overflow vulnerability

item.title: CVE-2025-7937 · 7.2 HIGH — Supermicro BMC firmware update validation bypass
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the Supermicro BMC firmware validation logic at Supermicro MBD-X12STW . An attacker can update the system firmware with a specially crafted image.

item.title: CVE-2025-6198 · 7.2 HIGH — Supermicro BMC firmware update validation bypass
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the Supermicro BMC firmware validation logic at Supermicro MBD-X13SEM-F . An attacker can update the system firmware with a specially crafted image.

item.title: CVE-2024-10239 · 7.2 HIGH — fld->used_bytes without sanity check causes stack overflow
item.description: A security issue in the firmware image verification implementation at Supermicro MBD-X12DPG-OA6 . An attacker with administrator privileges can upload a specially crafted image, which can cause a stack overflow due to the unchecked fat->fsd.max_fld.

item.title: CVE-2024-10238 · 7.2 HIGH — fld->used_bytes without sanity check causes stack overflow
item.description: A security issue in the firmware image verification implementation at Supermicro MBD-X12DPG-OA6. An attacker can upload a specially crafted image that will cause a stack overflow is caused by not checking fld->used_bytes.

item.title: CVE-2024-10237 · 7.2 HIGH — SMC BMC Firmware Image Authentication Design Issue
item.description: There is a vulnerability in the BMC firmware image authentication design at Supermicro MBD-X12DPG-OA6 . An attacker can modify the firmware to bypass BMC inspection and bypass the signature verification process
